name: Mint Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine vinegar, water, mint leaves, and enough green coloring to give the desired tint.
2. Add sugar, stir until dissolved.
3. Bring mixture to a boil.
4. Add pectin at once, stirring constantly, and bring back to a full boil for 1/2 minute.
5. Strain off mint leaves and pour into glasses.
6. When cold, cover with paraffin.
Approximate yield: 6 (6 oz.) glasses. My note: The newer directions call for the jelly to be sealed while scalding hot. I've also put lids on and put it in the freezer. If you have trouble with thickening, use more pectin.
